no_changes_rationale:
status: needs_investigation
reason: Parent bead ddx-256af8b5 was already decomposed in a prior execute-bead attempt (see prior no_changes event on 2026-05-02T23:08:30Z) into 4 children that cover the original ACs:
  - ddx-468d800f (S15-7a) — ADR-conformance lint for mutation resolvers (requireTrusted + CSRF/Origin).
  - ddx-eb75a32d (S15-7b) — Coordinator→client mutation routing envelope (foundation).
  - ddx-ba31fec8 (S15-7c) — Origin identity attestation forwarding + dual authorization. Depends on S15-7b.
  - ddx-ea1ada47 (S15-7d) — Integration test for coordinator→client operatorPromptSubmit with identity attestation. Depends on S15-7b and S15-7c.

All 4 children exist with status=open and dependency edges recorded. No further implementation belongs at the parent level — the parent is a tracking bead and should remain open until children close. Closing it now would orphan the children (which list ddx-256af8b5 as a dep).

This is a triage outcome, not an implementation gap. The bead should stay parked under triage:needs-investigation until either (a) children complete and the parent can be closed, or (b) human triage decides to invert the dep direction so children unblock independently.
